TextENGNew: That evening, there was a meeting of the Watanagashi festival executive committee.
It was held at the assembly hall on the Furude Shrine grounds.
I knew the building well by sight, but this was my first time actually going inside.
changeReason: Old says 'seen the building a few times' understating familiarity; Japanese よく知っていた means 'knew well,' which the new translation captures.

TextENGNew: The walls of the assembly hall were covered with framed photos and memorabilia celebrating the great victory of the dam conflict.
changeReason: Old omits the celebratory nature (偉業を称える) and variety of items (額縁や写真); new specifies framed photos and memorabilia celebrating the dam conflict victory.

TextENGNew: ...According to Mion, the building had served as the command center for the resistance movement during the dam war.
Most striking of all was the prominent display of a banner reading 'Onigafuchi Guardians,' covered in handwritten messages from countless supporters.
changeReason: Old omits the banner's prominent placement (一番目立つところ) and reduces many people's collective writings to 'People had written messages.'

TextENGNew: My invitation to the meeting was more of a meet-and-greet than anything else.
After all, I'd accepted the role of auction emcee but hadn't formally introduced myself to the committee yet.
changeReason: Old says 'first time attending a meeting' while Japanese says he hadn't introduced himself since accepting the emcee role; new is more accurate.

TextENGNew: About thirty committee members had gathered, and my father and I were introduced at the start of the meeting.
Since I was still a minor, I was officially participating under my parent's supervision.
changeReason: Old implies Keiichi brought his dad; Japanese specifies 'participating under parental supervision' (親の保護下での参加), which new correctly conveys.

TextENGNew: ...I'd often heard the term 'parental rights' recently, because of Satoko's situation, and because of that I'd been thinking about the relationship between me and my dad.
...The Maebara family is a pretty happy one, I think.
...I'm blessed to have parents like them.
``Hello. I'm Keiichi Maebara...@k@vS12/01/180100958. This is my first time doing something like this and I'm very nervous...@k@vS12/01/180100959. But I'll do my best!``
``Hear, hear!@k@vS12/00/manA18007. You do your best, young man!``
Their friendly smiles and applause made me realize again how welcoming people are in this village.
I felt pressured as I thought about how big of a responsibility I was going to undertake at the festival.
Since I looked so nervous, the leaders of the committee all came up to me and said that I could just act natural and do my thing.
``Thank you for offering my son such an important position.@k@vS12/00/182600026. I'm sorry that I'm unable to participate in council activities due to my job, but...
I will do what I can to support my son.``
Clap clap clap clap!!
My dad must be used to making speeches.
``All right then, we'll break up into groups and start the meeting.@k@vS12/00/shikai18002. It's going to last until 8:50 PM.@k@vS12/00/shikai18003. Please begin your discussions as soon as you can.``
The festival committee can be broken up in different teams, like a general affairs team, a construction team, a refreshment booth team, and a ceremony team.
Each team has separate meetings.
My dad and I were told to join the event team.
...But after all, we joined toward the end without knowing anything, and the festival was next Sunday too.
I didn't understand anything they were saying and I couldn't even express any opinions anyway.
Nodding and pretending to participate in the proceedings was the most we could do.
Near the end, I finally got the information about the auction I needed. I found out what time I was supposed to be backstage, and other stuff about my job like that.
They asked me what I was going to wear, and I stopped short...
I thought I could just wear my regular clothes, but they suggested that I should wear something more flashy, since I was the host of the event.@k ...But I don't have any clothes like that.
``How about a suit with a bowtie? That would make you look like a real MC!``
``A bowtie?@k@vS12/01/180100961. That's so embarrassing!!@k@vS12/01/180100962. I wanna wear something more casual than that!``
``How's the meeting going, Kei-chan?
What'cha talking about?``
``They're talking about what he's going to wear during the auction.@k@vS12/10/181000209. If you don't have anything flashy to wear, I can get you something like that.``
``Really?@k@vS12/00/182600030. Oh, I'm sorry I haven't introduced myself yet... I'm Keiichi's father.``

``Dad, I don't trust him to get me proper clothes...@k@vS12/01/180100964. I'm sure he won't get me anything for men...@k@vS12/01/180100965. A bowtie is fine.@k@vS12/01/180100966. I'll just wear that.``
If I didn't compromise on that, Irie and Mion would try and make me wear something outrageous.
...She'd also want me to wear women's clothes...
``You're part of the committee too, huh?@k@vS12/01/180100968. Which team are you on?``
``He's on the general affairs team, where he's in charge of first aid.@k@vS12/03/180300424. We always ask for his help, since he's a doctor and all.``

``It's an easy job. For the most part, I'm just drinking beer under the main tent.@k@vS12/10/181000212. It's nothing compared to your work, Maebara-san.@k@vS12/10/181000213. I'm looking forward to it!``
``Yaahahaha....``
``......I'm going to drink barley tea with him too.@k@vS12/05/180500666. Nipah~☆``
``You absolutely cannot, Furude-san!
Oh, good evening, Maebara-kun.@k@vS12/22/181400117. Nice to see you too, Maebara-san.``
``Ah, ah, hello, ma'am!@k@vS12/00/182600032. Thank you for being so nice to my son all the time...``
``Maebara-kun, are you ready for the auction?@k@vS12/00/183200029. I'm looking forward to seeing your show!``
It felt weird to see Chie-sensei and the principal outside of school.
``Rika-chan, you're performing a dance at the festival, right?@k@vS12/01/180100971. I heard it's right after my show. I'm looking forward to it!``
``It's a traditional ceremony that she's performing.@k@vS12/03/180300426. I'm sure you're nervous about it, huh.@k@vS12/03/180300427. How do you feel this year?``
``......I'm used to it now, so I'm fine.``
``You've only done it once last year, but you're already used to it?@k@vS12/03/180300429. I'm impressed!``
``Maebara-saaan!@k@vS12/11/180700098. It's been a while.``
``Oh, Ooishi-san!@k@vS12/01/180100973. It's nice to see you!``
``Have you been practicing the Tsubame Gaeshi?
Nfu fu!@k@vS12/11/180700101. We should play together!@k@vS12/11/180700102. You won't get better unless you play every day, you know?!``
``You know how to play mahjong, Maebara-kun?``
``Oh, he knows how to play, doesn't he?@k@vS12/11/180700104. Nfu fu fu!!@k@vS12/11/180700105. He's a pretty good player, too.``
``Heh heh heh...!@k@vS12/03/180300431. Perhaps we really should start playing mahjong in our club now!``
``......We should go and crush every mahjong parlor in the city.``
Ooishi-san thinks I'm an experienced player since I was practicing Tsubame Gaeshi the other day...
Why do people misunderstand me all the time...?
``By the way, Maebara-san.@k@vS12/10/181000216. I heard about that case...``
``What case?``

``About what you're planning to do tomorrow evening.@k@vS12/10/181000218. A few of my staff members and I are going to be there as well.@k@vS12/10/181000219. Takano-san and Tomitake-san are coming too.
We can't bring that many people, but I hope you'll let us join you.``
``That's... that's so nice of you!@k@vS12/01/180100976. Thank you so much!@k@vS12/01/180100977. Every person counts!``
``I'm sorry that we can't do much to help her, but we still want to do something.@k@vS12/10/181000222. This is what Takano-san suggested.``
``Yes.@k@vS12/10/181000224. She wanted to help somehow.@k@vS12/10/181000225. She feels bad that she wasn't able to assist you the other day.``
``Oh, are you talking about that rumor?@k@vS12/11/180700107. The one saying that Maebara-san's become the leader of a protest group or something?``
``Ahahahaha...@k@vS12/01/180100979. Even you know about it?@k@vS12/01/180100980. You know about Satoko?``
``Of course he does.@k@vS12/03/180300433. He's very sensitive to rumors inside the village.``
``Na ha ha... It's only my job.@k@vS12/11/180700109. I'm very aware of what's going on with Satoko-san...``
``Ooishi-san... What do you think about her case?``
Ooishi-san is, after all, a police officer.
Could he maybe have an option other than going to the child consultation center...?
``Well, I don't think the child consultation center is being unreasonable.@k@vS12/11/180700120. However, it must seem terrible from the point of view of those who know Satoko-san and Teppei-san.``
Ooishi-san was exactly right.
``I'm sure they're going to call him tonight because we told them that she's been missing school for three days.@k@vS12/03/180300436. They'll ask him why she hasn't gone to school.``
``So she might come to school tomorrow?!``

``...That's possible.@k@vS12/03/180300438. Teppei can't stay in Okinomiya for some reason.@k@vS12/03/180300439. That's why he needs to stay at Satoko's house.@k@vS12/03/180300440. I'm sure he doesn't want any further trouble.``
``He can't stay in Okinomiya for... some reason?``
``Na ha ha ha...@k@vS12/11/180700122. That's right, for some reason.@k@vS12/11/180700123. I heard that he had some kind of money-related trouble.
You don't know about that, Sonozaki-san?@k@vS12/11/180700125. Nfu fu fu...!``
``I have no idea...@k@vS12/03/180300442. I don't know what you're talking about~``
``What do you mean, Mion...?@k@vS12/01/180100984. There's a reason he wants to stay in Satoko's house?``
``Well... yeah... ahaha.@k@vS12/03/180300444. Teppei has money issues, so he can't live in Okinomiya anymore.@k@vS12/03/180300445. He's hiding out in Hinamizawa until the trouble brews over.``
``Then he should hide somewhere alone.@k@vS12/10/181000234. Why would he try and drag Satoko-chan into this!?``
``......You want Satoko as your maid, right? It's the same thing.``
``No!! That's not right!!
Maids are about love!
There is love behind my desires!!@k@vS12/10/181000238. I'm totally against violence!
A master should treat his maid with love!!``
``Let's just ignore that pervert for a moment.
So Ooishi-san... You think there's no choice but to let the child consultation center take care of it?``

``I think that's the most realistic plan...@k@vS12/11/180700127. There are certain steps that you need to follow.@k@vS12/11/180700128. When you take a piss, you go to the toilet and pull down your zipper.
If you don't follow those steps, it ends in disaster.``
``That's a gross way to put it.@k@vS12/03/180300449. You disgusting old man~``
``Na ha ha ha... I'm sorry I can't help you.
But I do find Satoko-san's case unfortunate.@k@vS12/11/180700132. I hope that everyone gets to have a happy ending.``

``The child consultation center is going to make their decision regardless, but what you guys are doing isn't against the law either.@k@vS12/10/181000241. What you're doing, Maebara-kun, isn't a mistake.
Don't give up. Do all you can do.@k@vS12/10/181000243. I'll support you too.``
``Thank you very much.@k@vS12/01/180100986. We went there yesterday and today, and we're going to go there tomorrow too.@k@vS12/01/180100987. I believe we can save her as long as we don't give up.``
``Hmmm... three days in row, huh?@k@vS12/11/180700134. I heard you took twenty people with you today.@k@vS12/11/180700135. Are you going to take that many tomorrow too?``
``Heh heh heh!@k@vS12/03/180300451. Kei-chan is really talented when it comes to stuff like this!``
``......You'll be surprised. There's going to be sixty people tomorrow!``

``My, that's amazing...@k@vS12/11/180700137. I can see you becoming an active labor union leader when you grow up.@k@vS12/11/180700138. The head of our union is pretty deplorable.@k@vS12/11/180700139. Na ha ha...!``
At that moment, I heard someone clapping.
It was almost 9 PM.
The meeting was over.
Everyone returned to their seats and listened to each section head's report on what they discussed.
``You're incredible, Keiichi...@k@vS12/00/182600034. You know so many people from the village.``
``But there are even more people that I don't know.``

``Are you sure?@k@vS12/00/182600036. You were talking with so many of them.@k@vS12/00/182600037. Mm-hmm, I really am impressed!@k@vS12/00/182600038. They're such nice people... Maybe I should be more involved with them too.``
``You should.@k@vS12/01/180100990. Hinamizawa is all about helping each other.@k@vS12/01/180100991. I know you're busy with your work, but you should be involved as much as you can.``
``I'm going to talk with your mother about that, I think.``
``That's it for the meeting today.@k@vS12/00/shikai18005. The next meeting is... oh, tomorrow!@k@vS12/00/shikai18006. I'll see you all then.
It will be the last major meeting before the festival.@k@vS12/00/shikai18008. Thank you for coming today.@k@vS12/00/shikai18009. Chairman, will you give us some closing remarks?``
``No, it's fine. Let's just go home and get some drinks!@k@vS12/18/183300002. That's it for now.@k@vS12/18/183300003. Let's meet tomorrow at the same time.@k@vS12/18/183300004. Thank you!``
Wha ha ha ha!
Everyone laughed at Mayor Kimiyoshi's closing remarks.
We finished cleaning up, and everyone began filing out of the building.
Even though it was a weekday, some of them were going out for drinks.
They asked my dad to go along with them.
...My dad isn't an ordinary worker.@k He can sleep in if he wants to, as long as he makes his deadlines.
``Keiichi...@k@vS12/00/182600041. They want me to come for a drink, so I'm going, okay?``
``Sure, have fun.@k@vS12/01/180100993. I'm heading home now.``
Going home alone was kind of sad, so I thought I'd walk home with Mion, but she was still lingering at the meeting talking with the mayor and other important people.
She was probably busy, with the festival being so near.
As I contemplated whether to wait for her or not, Ooishi-san came up to me.
``Good job at the meeting.@k@vS12/11/180700141. How did you get here?@k@vS12/11/180700142. I can give you a ride home if you want.``
``I came here on my bike.@k@vS12/01/180100995. Plus, I'm waiting for Mion.``
``I see, I see.@k@vS12/11/180700144. You'll be waiting a while, though.@k@vS12/11/180700145. They're discussing the trouble they had with the refreshment booth team.``
``Trouble with the refreshment booth team?@k@vS12/01/180100997. It seems like such a fun festival, but they still have some issues, huh?``

``Oh, yes.@k@vS12/11/180700147. It's hard to make adjustments this late.@k@vS12/11/180700148. Shaved ice and yakisoba are always popular, so everyone wants to do those.@k@vS12/11/180700149. But it'd be a problem if all the booths sold yakisoba, right?

They need to make those kind of adjustments so there will be a variety of booths, but...@k@vS12/11/180700151. People get upset when they have to run a booth that's not going to make much money, you know?

They suggest running the booths in rotation every year, but some people don't have enough staff to do certain kinds of booths, and other people claim that their yakisoba is better than everyone else's...
They also fight about locations, and who to hire, et cetera, et cetera.``
``I had no idea that the preparations for a festival were so complicated...``
``Everything needs adjustments.@k@vS12/11/180700155. I guess it sounds like boring adult talk for a young man like yourself.

But adjustments are necessary to make things go smoothly.@k@vS12/11/180700157. What you're doing at the child consultation center is a secret adjustment, in a way.@k@vS12/11/180700158. Satoko-san and Teppei-san don't even know about it.``
``Ugh... I guess... you might be right...@k@vS12/01/180101000. But we have no choice but to raise our voices.@k@vS12/01/180101001. That's the only option we've got.``
``No, no, I'm proud to see you do something like this.@k@vS12/11/180700160. If I was your age, all I'd be able to think of is attacking him in the dark...
Ah ha ha ha ha... Man, ha ha ha.``
Ooishi must have had no other plans. He stuck around while I waited for Mion.
There was no one else around at that time of night.
It would've felt pretty lonely if he hadn't been there with me.@k ...I'm glad Ooishi stayed to chat.
We talked about mahjong for a while.
Its theory, techniques, and history.
I rarely talked with a guy his age, so it was very interesting for me.
It's nice to interact with people from a different generation sometimes.
...He's also a police officer.
I was secretly hoping that he'd give me a hint about how to save Satoko.
...In any case, I had to wonder what was taking Mion so long.
By the time Ooishi-san finished his second cigarette, I began to think that maybe I should just go home for the night...
``I think I'm gonna head home.@k@vS12/01/180101003. Mion is taking too long...@k@vS12/01/180101004. ...I have something big to take care of after school tomorrow.``
Ooishi took out his third cigarette.
That meant he was going to stay here until he finished it.
I didn't feel like waiting here with him, though.
I just wanted to go home and relax so I could prepare for tomorrow.
``I'm going home now.``
``You know, Maebara-san.@k@vS12/11/180700172. ...There's something I wanted to tell you.``
The tone of his voice had changed slightly.
Whatever it was, it sounded important.@k I stopped and turned back around to face him.
``Actually, you see...@k@vS12/11/OISHI081803. ...I don't think you should go to the child consultation center tomorrow. You've made your point already.``
``...But... we need to do this so we can save Satoko.@k@vS12/01/180101013. They aren't getting how urgent the situation is.
We have to make even more of a fuss if that's what it takes for them to understand.@k@vS12/01/180101015. I think we'll make a bigger impact if we go there three days in row.``
``You're trying to show them every courtesy, huh?@k@vS12/11/180700176. Na ha ha.
...But you know, Maebara-san.@k@vS12/11/180700178. Sometimes, you should just let sleeping dogs lie.``
``Ah, let me make this clear.@k@vS12/11/180700180. This isn't a warning from the police, and no one told me to tell you this.
I'm just giving you advice... as your mahjong friend.``
The word 'advice' didn't sound pleasant at all...@k ...I felt myself frowning involuntarily.
``Not quite.@k@vS12/11/OISHI081805. They've started making secret adjustments so that you can't come by to pressure them anymore...``
``Wh-What do you mean...?``
They made a secret adjustment so that I couldn't bother them any more?
I felt confused... and then Ooishi-san said something that almost sounded like a line from a political drama.
``The consultation center is simply hoping that you'll give up...@k@vS12/11/180700227. ......They have other cases that need more urgent attention.
......But they're not sure yet if they should ask you to give up.@k@vS12/11/180700229. ......That's what I wanted to tell you today.``
``Because you were so well-organized, they're suspecting that you're not the actual leader... and that the Sonozaki family are the ones behind this movement.
I'm sure you know how powerful the Sonozaki family is in this village...``

``I do...@k@vS12/01/180101037. But it has nothing to do with the Sonozaki family!@k@vS12/01/180101038. We're simply fighting to save our friend.@k@vS12/01/180101039. Mion hasn't even mentioned her name...``
``That's what the child consultation center wanted to know.@k@vS12/11/180700241. It's their normal routine to politely refuse a huge crowd of petitioners.

However... things would be different if this was Oryou Sonozaki-san's idea.@k@vS12/11/180700243. Oryou-san holds great sway in the city's government.@k@vS12/11/180700244. No one can go against her wishes.``
``I'm aware that Mion's grandmother is very powerful, but that has nothing to do with this!``
``In order to make that clear, the local government officials have approached the Sonozaki family in secret.@k@vS12/11/180700246. They met Oryou-san and asked her about this case.
They were going to take action if they found out that it was her idea,@k@vS12/11/180700248. but they soon discovered otherwise.``
``So...... they're not going to take any action anymore?``
``It's not just that.@k@vS12/11/180700250. Oryou-san is now aware that there's a commotion going on within the village.
I'm not sure if you're aware of this, but some people in the village still hold a grudge against the Houjou family, the traitors of the dam conflict.

Ever since the curse of Oyashiro-sama, the Sonozaki family has been telling people not to interact with the Houjou family.@k@vS12/11/180700253. ......You might wake up a sleeping dog if you continue to make all this noise.``
``What happens if I wake the dog up?``
``I don't know...``
Ooishi paused for a while and drew on his short cigarette.
``I'm not saying that you'll be threatened or that they will hurt you...
However... You just moved here, and you're starting to get to know people from the village... but soon enough, you might get to see the underside of this place.``
...The issues between the Houjou family and the Sonozaki family...@k Well, village-wide matters have been slightly taboo in our group, but... we may have to go down that road now.
It's a fact that we haven't got any support from adults, except for the school teachers and Irie.
Other adults in the village must know what happened between Satoko and Teppei last year.
However, no adult came forward to help us.
``If we continue our movement, will the people who hate the Houjou family become our enemies too...?``
``That's possible.@k@vS12/11/180700258. The Sonozaki family may get involved if you continue this commotion.@k@vS12/11/180700259. ...You could say that the Sonozaki family is anti-Houjou.
If you try to save Satoko-san using people from Hinamizawa, that shows huge disrespect towards the Sonozaki family. It could create a huge problem...``
By that point, I knew.@k This was a warning, not just advice.
An ignorant young man in Hinamizawa is making noise, trying to wake up a sleeping dog.
``I know you've taken a liking to this place, and that you're really doing your best here.
That's why...... I thought I should let you know about this.@k@vS12/11/180700263. I'm sorry if I upset you.``
I love Hinamizawa.
I've made a decision to make this place my home.
I'm motivated about the big job I got for the festival too.
...Would it really be wise for me to try and create all this trouble?
``I'm just speaking as your friend, nothing more.@k@vS12/11/180700265. I'm not trying to threaten you or anything like that.
I'm not trying to make you do anything you don't want to.@k@vS12/11/180700267. I just wanted to give you this information.``
Ooishi smiled at me... then stomped on his cigarette.
...I thought I had just made the decision to do what was right.
But was that only the stupid idea of an ignorant little boy? ...Did I need to grow up right now, and think about this again?
...Dark clouds hung over my heart.
But... wait a minute.
...Did that mean I didn't even have the courage to risk losing something?
Was I not brave enough to save Satoko at all?
I had to be the red fire.
I couldn't give up, even if everybody else did.
I had to fill other people up with righteous flames, just like I was!
``What's the matter?
Nfu fu!@k@vS12/11/180700270. What a bold smile you're wearing right now.``
``Thank you for the valuable advice.
But... I can't give up, no matter what you tell me.@k@vS12/01/180101048. I won't stop even if the whole world tells me to.``
``Even if the whole world is against you... huh.@k@vS12/11/180700272. You're a stronger man than I thought.``

``If the whole world is telling me that I'm making a mistake, I'll have to stop and re-evaluate myself.@k@vS12/01/180101050. However, I've already talked about Satoko's case with my friends over and over again.
This isn't just my idea.@k@vS12/01/180101052. I'm confident that our decision is the very best one to make!``
``I agree with you, Maebara-san.@k@vS12/11/180700274. Young people like you tend to make hasty and dangerous decisions.@k@vS12/11/180700275. However, all of you are being very rational about this.
You're making a democratic appeal.@k@vS12/11/180700277. Just like Dr. Irie was saying, there's nothing wrong with your methods.``
``I know!@k@vS12/01/180101054. I'm positive that we're not making any mistakes.@k@vS12/01/180101055. We're doing our best to save our friend.
We're not going to give up just because we feel threatened. We are her true comrades.@k@vS12/01/180101057. I have faith in us, even if the rest of the world doesn't.
......That's what friendship is all about!``
``Hmm...@k@vS12/11/180700279. You have a strong will.@k@vS12/11/180700280. Not even my detectives are as strong as you are...``
Ooishi-san gave me a wicked grin of admiration.
I could tell from that grin.
He wasn't trying to pressure me at all.
It was exactly the opposite of that.
He had shared this information with me to confirm that I was ready to face this challenge.
I didn't even have to consider whether his words were advice or a warning.

``Every man has many things he wants to keep believing in, even if others don't share that belief.@k@vS12/01/180101060. ...Of course it's important to listen to advice with sincerity, but some beliefs you still can't turn away from.
That's what being a man is all about.``
``Nfu fu fu fu!@k@vS12/11/180700282. I also have a strong will, you know...
About five years ago, a person who was like a brother to me was killed.``
``H-He was murdered...?``
``The case was declared solved, but... nfu fu fu.@k@vS12/11/180700285. I'm continuing an investigation alone, because I suspect there's another person in control behind the scenes.
